purpose: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game dialogue.
format: JSONL: one JSON object per line. Dialogue objects use record_type='dialogue_sample'.

voice_profile.role: Legal structure; dry mentor; readiness checker; case architect; professional spine of Pig & Swine.
voice_profile.core_voice: Calm, precise, professional, and mildly cynical. Murrow turns panic into issues, facts, evidence, procedure, and remedy. His jokes are small, dry, and usually sound like filing advice with disappointment attached.
avoid: Do not make Murrow panicked; that is Mr. Pig's function.
Do not make him flamboyant; that is Whimsy's function.
Do not make him merely cold; he cares, but expresses care as structure and risk management.
Do not over-explain legal doctrine where a practical checklist will do.
Do not make every line sarcastic; his cynicism should be controlled and useful.
voice_profile.arc: Chapter 1 explains the basic case loop; Chapter 2 teaches structured evidence; Chapter 3 translates printer chaos into access-to-court sabotage; Chapter 4 organises Swine's disaster into arbitrable issues; Chapter 5 architects the final brief and lets a small amount of pride show.

speaker: Murrow
context: generic greeting at the file area
text: Dr. A. Cula. If Mr. Pig has already briefed you, assume the facts are underneath the smoke.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: repeat interaction when no new quest is active
text: Nothing is presently on fire. In this office, that is not calm; it is a drafting window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: player asks what to do next without active quest
text: Start with the file. When the file fails you, start with the person. When the person fails you, check the deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: You appear to have converted one problem into several errands. That is usually how legal work begins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A second conversation rarely changes the law. It may, however, reveal where Asia put the relevant folder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Evidence is not decoration. If it does not support an issue, it is paper with self-esteem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Before court, ask three things: what happened, what rule matters, and what remedy we can honestly request.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The court will tolerate one strong point better than six wounded metaphors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: comment after Cula collects irrelevant object
text: Interesting. Not useful, but interesting has ruined many afternoons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: comment after player finds a useful clue
text: Good. That is a fact with a future.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: comment when Mr. Pig is panicking nearby
text: Mr. Pig is not wrong that there is a crisis. He is merely wrong about its shape, volume, and likely weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: comment on Asia managing the office
text: Asia knows where the files are, which makes her the closest thing this firm has to infrastructure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Crab distrusts documents until they earn it. A useful habit, if not a cheerful one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Whimsy is valuable when aimed. Unaimed, he becomes weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: comment on Swine before he returns
text: Mr. Swine communicates by postcard because postcards cannot be cross-examined.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The office is still absurd. The difference is that now the absurdity has a filing system.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Cula finds Murrow after Pig panic
text: Dr. A. Cula, I assume Mr. Pig has explained the case as a financial weather event. Let us locate the law underneath it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The client may have lost a procedural chance because service was defective. That is the problem. Everything else is office acoustics.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We do not need to prove the entire dispute today. We need to prove the client should have been allowed into it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The file is incomplete, which is regrettable, common, and not yet fatal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: quest update for binder and memo
text: Find the procedural binder and the rights memo. One tells us the rule. The other reminds the court why the rule matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: player asks why Pig cannot explain case
text: Mr. Pig understands consequences. He should not be required to understand sequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The binder should be in the archive, unless it has finally achieved personhood and resigned.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: player enters archive with Murrow hint active
text: Look for the object that appears too heavy to be merely administrative. That will be the binder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: No. That is tax procedure. Useful in the abstract, but rarely as a rescue device.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: There it is. Heavy, dull, and therefore more reliable than most of us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The binder gives us the defective-service argument. Please carry it in the legal sense, not necessarily the physical one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Now we have a rule. We still need the rights point, unless we intend to argue fairness by facial expression.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Crab should look at the address and service details. He will complain, but in a technically productive manner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Crab requires proof that you are not another first-day optimism casualty. The binder may satisfy him. It satisfies very little else.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Excellent. The service point now has facts, not just suspicion in a suit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The rights memo may be with Whimsy. He collects fair-hearing arguments the way other people collect receipts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Find Whimsy. If he is speaking loudly, wait for the legal part and interrupt before the pastry metaphor spreads.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Fair hearing is now more than atmosphere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Whimsy has joined. That improves our rhetoric and increases our need for supervision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: You appear alert. Try to spend that alertness on legal sequence rather than hand gestures.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: You are moving faster than the file. That is rarely ideal, but at least you are not asleep.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Let us check the case before the court checks it for us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Without the binder, we can argue service only from memory and courage. Courts prefer sources.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Without the rights memo, fairness becomes vibes. Judges dislike vibes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Crab has the service point. Find him before the court asks the obvious question.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We need someone who can make fair hearing sound less like homework. Unfortunately, that is Whimsy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We have rule, right, fact, and remedy. That is not certainty, but it is a case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Once inside, answer the question asked. Not the question Mr. Pig fears. Not the question Whimsy prefers. The question asked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The first issue is why the default should not stand. Start with service. Procedure is the doorway.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. The court is listening. Do not celebrate; listening is not judgment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: That was colourful. Try lawful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Now explain why defective service matters. A missed envelope is not the point. Exclusion from the process is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Yes. Procedure is not decoration. It is access.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Ask for the modest remedy. Courts trust counsel who know when not to ask for the moon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A reset. Not triumph. Better: a lawful opportunity. The distinction matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Against several predictions, including some of mine, we now have a coherent result.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The firm survives. Temporarily, which is the only type of survival I have observed here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A postcard from Mr. Swine. The stamp is expensive. The contents are worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: File the judgment. Label the evidence. Enjoy the solvent interval while it remains theoretical.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A firm that survived yesterday should avoid optimism this morning. Optimism attracts clients with papers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Sit down, Waldek. Start with the paper you received, then the thing they say you did, then the thing they are trying to take.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The poster is not the case. The eviction is the case. The poster is how the landlord hopes to make the eviction sound tidy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Home, expression, notice, proportionality, factual basis. For one poster, that is an ambitious amount of law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Visit Waldek's apartment. Inspect the notice, the poster, and the letters. Then ask whether anyone was actually disturbed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The poster matters because the landlord says it matters. That is usually when evidence becomes necessary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Start at the home. Eviction cases should not begin in abstraction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This district is quieter than the office. That does not mean the problem is smaller.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This is not just an address. Courts sometimes need reminding of that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A poster. No claws, no engine, no immediate threat to masonry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Severe consequence, vague allegation, few dates. The notice is doing volume where it should do particulars.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The warnings become harsher while becoming less specific. A familiar administrative species.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We need witnesses. If the alleged disturbance existed, it should have inconvenienced someone other than the landlord's vocabulary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Speak to Kowalski. He notices things no one asked him to notice. Occasionally, that helps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Kowalski is establishing a broad evidential background of irritation. Guide him gently toward relevance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. No disturbance from the person most qualified to be disturbed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Zielinska may know whether the rule was applied generally or merely pointed at Waldek.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Selective enforcement is not a rule problem. It is a rule revealing itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We have no real disturbance and possible targeting. Now find the rule they claim justifies the result.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The room appears to have been designed by someone who distrusts curtains with opinions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The anti-poster sign is, regrettably, a poster. I will try not to enjoy that in court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Façade disharmony. A phrase with confidence, reach, and no obvious boundary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Counsel. Beige is not a legal standard, even where sincerely held.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: If there were complaints, there should be dates, names, and content. If there are only adjectives, we have a problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The complaint file is near an open window. That is either negligent filing or narrative inevitability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Dates after the notice. Duplicated complaints. The chronology has injured itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Damaged, but legible enough to be troublesome. That is often sufficient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The complaint record has become weather. Fortunately, weather was not our only witness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: return to office for evidence board
text: Return to the office. We have documents; now we must convert them into an argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A case is not a pile of documents. A case is an argument that knows where its documents are.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Notice, rule, facts, proportionality. Four questions. Do not let Whimsy add a fifth called moral thunder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. The notice is where the vagueness begins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The poster matters, but it does not prove what the notice failed to say.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Yes. An overbroad rule is useful only if we show the breadth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: That is evidence of consequence. We need evidence of the supposed rule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. The alleged disturbance now has a factual problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Yes. The court must see the gap between the conduct and the consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This case is legally alive, but limping. We can proceed, though I would prefer fewer missing limbs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good enough for court. Not elegant. Courts rarely require elegance, despite their furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Notice, rule, facts, consequence. The case now has a spine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Remember: do not argue that posters are good. Argue that eviction was not a lawful answer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Grzyb will defend the rule. Ask what it actually means before anyone treats it as holy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. A rule without limits cannot carry a home out of the room.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Now test the alleged disturbance. A complaint record is evidence only if it behaves like one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The factual basis is now less solid than the landlord's sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The final issue is proportion. Match the minor conduct against the severe consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Temporary protection. Not perfect, but Waldek sleeps in his own home tonight. That is not small.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The eviction is stopped. The law performed its least glamorous function: it slowed harm down long enough to see it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A strong result. The court saw vagueness, targeting, and disproportion in one file. Rarely pleasant, but useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The poster survives. The office survives. I will not speculate which is more structurally stable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The printer made a noise after the judgment. I dislike mechanical foreshadowing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Before anyone suggests exorcism, printers do not acquire intent. They acquire faults. Sometimes faults acquire accomplices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Pig, the printer is not our opponent. At most, it is a confused witness with toner issues.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This is not merely a printing problem. If filings vanish before deadlines, it becomes an access-to-court problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Inspect the printer, the deadline notice, and the misprinted filing. Then we determine whether incompetence had assistance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A short deadline turns inconvenience into prejudice. That is why timing matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A blank pleading is rarely persuasive, even in minimalist jurisdictions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Call Bajtek. I prefer technical explanations from people who do not ask machines about their feelings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Bajtek will explain the system. We will translate the explanation into something a court can accept without a diagram of despair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This room contains more blinking lights than legal certainty. Proceed carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Repeated redirection at the same time. Random failure rarely keeps appointments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. A backup is a witness that expected betrayal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We will test a statement against evidence. Select the line that cannot survive the log.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: If Bajtek says random, show him the pattern. Experts also need exhibits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Not random. Repeated. Scheduled. That gives the court something firmer than office folklore.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: That evidence is interesting, but it does not contradict the statement. Interesting is not the same as useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Find who had access to printer maintenance. Sabotage usually leaves invoices, because villains also outsource.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Compared with this place, our printer is a kettle with grievances.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A rival firm paid for maintenance on equipment it claims not to know. That is a sentence with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Advocate Szpon. Your timing is either unfortunate or evidential.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: He denies connection. Present the document that gives the denial a short life expectancy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. The invoice places him near the machinery he describes as theatrical weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: No. That proves malfunction. We need connection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The logs show redirection. The invoice shows access. Now find the physical source inside our own building.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The basement has the air of a place where files go to become rumours.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A clean cable in this basement is more suspicious than a dirty one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: There. Not a haunted printer. A manipulated route. The distinction may disappoint Mr. Pig, but it will assist the court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We do not have a printer problem. We have an access-to-court problem with wires.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Electronic filing is compromised. Prepare physical delivery. I regret how often emergency becomes transport.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Deliver the filing. Keep the pages together. If a pigeon signs anything, reject it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Clean delivery. The filing arrives with dignity, which is more than I expected from a scooter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The filing has tyre marks, but it also has a timestamp. We will emphasise the latter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Messy, late-looking, and still recoverable. This is now a sabotage argument with cardio.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: First establish abnormality. The court must understand this was not ordinary office incompetence, however tempting that theory may be.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Ordinary incompetence is random. This was punctual.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Now link the irregularity to the rival firm. Without causation, outrage is just weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Invoice, access, device. The chain is now visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The remedy is acceptance or restoration of the filing. Do not ask the court to solve printers generally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The filing is accepted provisionally. Thin, but breathing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The filing is accepted. Sabotage will not profit today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The court saw the pattern, the access, and the prejudice. That is as close to elegant as this printer will allow.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The printer has been exonerated. I suggest an apology short enough not to jam the tray.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: It printed THANK YOU. I refuse to treat that as testimony.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Something arrived from Mr. Swine. It has a bill attached, which is not a comforting genre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: An international arbitration notice before lunch. The day is maturing badly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Swine. Please place the gifts on the table, the receipts in chronological order, and the evasions where I can see them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We need four answers: contract, authority, charges, and what actually happened in Japan.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Swine's account contains snow, politeness, and a clipboard. We will need more than atmosphere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Review the arbitration notice and invoice. Then reconstruct the trip before the claimant reconstructs it for us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The claim is large enough to bankrupt the firm and vague enough to annoy me. An efficient combination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Seventeen puddings. Either an accounting error or a cry for help.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Start at the airport. Travel records are dull, which is why they often tell the truth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Airports are proof that procedure can exist without clarity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: No commercial intent, according to the form. A small fact, but small facts frequently survive cross-examination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The arrival board does what Mr. Swine does not: it provides a date without decoration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A lost suitcase containing legal documents is not a filing system. It is, however, on brand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The travel record helps. Now we need the words. Visit the Embassy and obtain a reliable translation note.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Professional quiet. Everyone behave as though Whimsy has been placed under seal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Ms. Tanaka, thank you. We need precision, which this file has been avoiding internationally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Courtesy is not commitment. That distinction may save several accounts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The appointment log supports the sequence. Mr. Swine may have stumbled into relevance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: For the record, the confusion belongs to Mr. Swine, not to translation as a profession.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: If there was an earlier draft, it will matter. Search Mr. Swine's records before his memory edits them again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This apartment explains several invoices and raises several moral questions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The expense report appears to have been assembled during turbulence, possibly emotional.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: There. The earlier draft lacks the frightening clause. Someone added legal teeth after the smile.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This transfer is not luxury spending. We will ask about it later, when the room contains fewer ski goggles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Swine, evasiveness is not privilege. It is usually just delay with cologne.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: If memory must be recovered downhill, at least collect the legally relevant fragments before the puddings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. He remembers the ceremonial memorandum. A rare case of skiing improving the record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A clipboard, a toast, and fear. Not ideal, but recognisable as evidence if handled gently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The memory is mostly snow and dessert. We will rely more heavily on documents, as civilisation intended.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Dr. A. Cula argues law. Mr. Swine explains facts. Mr. Pig remains decorative unless insolvency becomes relevant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Choose the speaker according to the question. Contract formation is law. Pudding quantity is, unfortunately, fact.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Dr. A. Cula with the draft and translation note. That is an argument, not a recollection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: wrong prep Swine on legal issue
text: Mr. Swine should not lead on contract formation. He once treated a menu as negotiable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Yes. Swine can explain the charges because, regrettably, he met the puddings personally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We can proceed, but the case is leaning heavily on documents and prayer. I prefer documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The case is ready. It is not clean. Clean cases do not arrive with ski goggles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Contract, authority, invoice, remedy. Even Mr. Swine now resembles a client rather than a weather system.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Answer in order. The tribunal will not appreciate improvisational chronology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Contract formation first. Courtesy language cannot be invoiced as obligation without proof.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Politeness has survived without becoming a debt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Now authority. Show that travel and hospitality did not bind the firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The firm is not liable merely because one partner was scenic abroad.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Invoice reliability. Let Swine explain the facts. Keep him away from legal philosophy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The pudding count has legal consequences. I will need time with that sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Remedy. The tribunal need not decide whether Swine was wise. Only whether the claimant proved the obligation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The major claim fails, but accounting remains. Alive, but invoiced.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Pig & Swine is not liable. Mr. Swine may still owe money to dessert, but that is a separate proceeding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A clean result from an unclean file. Do not expect this often.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Some of these transfers are not indulgence. They look like support for cases that could not pay.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A mass notice. Citywide. When paper arrives in bulk, rights usually leave individually.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The office is quiet. I distrust quiet here; it usually means paper is gathering elsewhere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Generic notices, different recipients, identical consequences. Administrative efficiency has developed teeth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Pig, a closure notice is not a compliment, however many fonts it uses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This is no longer one defective file. It is a process built to prevent individual files from mattering.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We need the plan, the notices, the objections, the conflict, the impact, and proof that this firm serves more than itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Start with the people affected. Abstract harm is easy to ignore; actual lives are less convenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This is what the notices do when they leave the printer and enter a life.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Consequences, in people's own words. The file is now heavier in the correct way.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Waldek's case was not isolated. It was a preview.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: If Kowalski cannot determine whether he has appealed, complied, or moved, the notice may have a clarity problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Zielinska is right. Treating different situations the same can be as arbitrary as treating the same situation differently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Access to justice includes the places people actually find lawyers, advice, and coffee strong enough to continue.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: We have impact. Now find the administrative machinery that pretends impact was assessed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: City Hall has the atmosphere of a place where responsibility goes to be formatted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The plan promises access to justice while removing the people currently providing it. Ambitious, in the way sinkholes are ambitious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Identical notices for different situations. Individual assessment appears to have been replaced by copy-paste with confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The contractor profits when the noticed people disappear. That is not background. That is motive with stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: He says each case was individually assessed. Present the evidence that all individuals received the same administrative face.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. If every assessment is identical, assessment may have been ceremonial.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: That shows harm. We need the template that shows the process ignored difference.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The notices were generic. Now prove objections were filtered before anyone heard them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This is not merely a printer. It is a procedural bottleneck with a paper tray.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Objections marked duplicate before review. Automation did not remove discretion. It hid it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: One command, hundreds of consequences. Efficiency is not a defence if judgment was never exercised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: I prefer injunctions to physical combat with stationery, but the law has chosen its arena.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Clean shutdown, logs preserved, objections recovered. I will pretend this was litigation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Messy shutdown. Enough proof remains, though the file now has battle damage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Emergency shutdown. The evidence is thinner, but it still shows a system designed not to listen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This is not a ski expense ledger. It is a record of cases kept alive when clients could not pay.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Swine, hiding generosity inside bad accounting is still bad accounting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: His secrecy was foolish. The work it funded may be essential. The court will need the second fact more than the first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A final brief is not a pile of good points. It is a route through a hostile forest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Defective notice, individual impact, technical suppression, conflict, proportionality, social utility, closing. Assign people to what they actually know.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: I can handle legal structure. It is less glamorous than thunder, which is why it tends to work.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Crab on causation. Good. He follows paper the way other people follow music.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Bajtek should explain the filter. Courts prefer technical cruelty in plain language.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: assign Whimsy to technical suppression wrong
text: Whimsy should not explain algorithms. The dragon-spreadsheet theory will not survive questions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Pig can speak to why people come here. Not accounts. Not procurement. Human doors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Swine can explain the pro-bono funding, provided he resists turning confession into travel writing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Waldek should speak to individual impact. A home is not an administrative unit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: assign Grzyb optional to procedural regularity
text: Grzyb is useful precisely because he likes rules. Even beige has limits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The final brief is thin, but breathing. We can proceed if the court is patient and the printer remains defeated.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The brief is viable. It will not sing, but it should stand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Law, facts, witnesses, motive, prejudice, remedy. The final brief has a spine and most of its limbs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: At the Supreme Court, jokes must travel with purpose. The bench will not tolerate decorative panic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This room is designed to make arguments feel small. Do not let it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Round one: notice and access. A document no ordinary recipient can understand is not notice; it is decoration with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Good. Procedural validity is now in serious doubt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Round two: proportionality. Public interest is not a spell that makes individual lives disappear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The court sees the burden, not just the plan.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Round three: suppression. Let the logs show what the system did before anyone could be heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Automation has lost its neutrality costume.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Round four: conflict. The same actors who process disappearance profit from disappearance. Make that visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The procurement link now has legal weight.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Final round: remedy. Suspend the scheme, restore objections, require individual assessment, preserve access to justice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Use the ledger carefully. Pig & Swine's value is not respectability. It is access.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A temporary injunction. Thin, but it keeps the door open. Sometimes law begins as a pause.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The scheme is suspended pending review. The district survives long enough to be treated individually.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A strong order: objections restored, assessment required, conflict exposed. Even this office may enjoy one measured breath.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The office is still shabby. Good. Sudden elegance would have raised enforcement questions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Everyone did the thing they were good at. This is rare, and I intend to document it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Mr. Pig has begun a speech. If it becomes nautical, someone please record only the legally relevant parts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: WELCOME BACK, COUNSEL. The printer has developed sentiment. I will not put that in the minutes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Pig & Swine remains open. Not because it is efficient. Because people still need a door that opens.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The facts are small. The consequence is not. That is where procedure begins to matter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: If the archive resists, remember: most binders are emotionally larger than physically necessary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Whimsy will say rights are soup. Extract the principle and leave the soup.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Do not ask for full victory. A fair reset is stronger because it is honest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The landlord has dressed a preference as a rule and a rule as a basis for eviction. We should undress that carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: You cannot measure the seriousness of eviction by the size of the apartment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Let Kowalski complain. Then listen for the one complaint he does not make.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The board is not improved by symmetry. It is improved by relevance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: You are arguing sympathy. Try proportionality. Sympathy has no filing stamp.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The printer may be innocent. Please prepare Mr. Pig emotionally for that possibility.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: A pattern is a fact trying to introduce itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Counsel, coincidences are permitted. In clusters, they become a theory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Do not argue that the printer suffered. Argue that the party did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Please answer in sentences that contain fewer resorts and more dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: This note separates politeness from liability. Civilisation depends on such distinctions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: You have chosen Swine to explain doctrine. The tribunal appears to be ageing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The speaker choice hurt us, but the document is strong. Recover through the exhibit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Do not assign people to where they are funniest. Assign them to where they are true.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Public interest becomes less persuasive when the private beneficiary is standing behind it with an invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: The remedy must stop the unlawful process, not redesign the city by applause.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: For once, everyone was useful in the correct order. That is almost management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 case framing after Cula finds Murrow
text: The hearing is at 14:00.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; Murrow frames playable objective; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
text: The notice of hearing was marked delivered.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; no reassurance or Pig commentary; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
text: The address on the notice does not match the client's address.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; concrete procedural defect; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 case framing: missing proof of receipt
text: The file lacks proof that anyone at the correct address received it.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; archival neutrality held; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 case framing: procedural binder authority
text: The procedural binder has Article 132 of the Code of Civil Procedure.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; English legal vocabulary per pack; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 case framing: rights memo objective
text: The rights memo matters after you prove the service problem.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; objective framing; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 answer to Cula's specific address-mismatch question
text: It is enough to start there.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; precise query receives precise answer; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 canonical Mr. Murrow to Murrow invitation
text: It is Murrow, to friends. The 'Mister' I keep for invoices.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 canonical line; faintly amused, not warm-welcoming; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
text: I am still deciding whether reception staff qualify as friends or as workplace constellations.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 canonical line; plants Asia running joke; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 directs Cula to procedural binder
text: Binder. Archive shelf, green spine, second row.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 draft; mechanical pointer to Beat 4; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — opening welcome to Cula
text: Doctor Cula. Welcome to Pig & Swine. You've picked a noisy day to begin.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; faintly amused, not warm-welcoming; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — human-stake frame line introducing Mrs. Borowski (pass-3 addition)
text: The case is a tenancy dispute. Mrs. Borowski moved out of the flat two years ago; the landlord has been pursuing rent at the old address ever since.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; pass-3 addition gives client a face; plain-language frame; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — first procedural defect: service
text: Two procedural defects in the complaint. The first is service. The notice was sent to the old address.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; joinder cut; service tightened (frame above carries the two-year fact); not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — second procedural defect: response window
text: The second is the response window. Filed inside the statutory limit if you count properly.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; two-defect floor; not final.

speaker: Murrow
text: The procedural binder is on the second shelf, blue spine. The rights memo is clipped inside the front cover. Hearing is at fourteen hundred, courtroom 4.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; mechanical pointer; supersedes earlier 'green spine' draft; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — answers Cula's address-on-file question
text: It is. Filed eighteen months ago in another matter. Same panel.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; precise short answer; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — first-name register invitation
text: It is Murrow, to friends. The 'Mister' I keep for invoices.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; canonical address-form invitation; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — hedges on whether reception staff are 'friends'
text: I am still deciding whether reception staff qualify as friends or as workplace constellations.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; canonical Asia-hedging line; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 3 V1.2 pass 3 — closing logistical availability line
text: Binder first. Memo second. The defects matter in that order. Find me before fourteen hundred if anything in the binder reads wrong.
notes: Phase 7 V1.2 pass 3 draft; logistical not reassuring; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — readiness check opens with one-word recognition
text: Cula.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; first-name register; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — confirms procedural binder on file
text: Procedural binder. On file.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; checklist register; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — confirms rights memo on file
text: Rights memo. On file.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; checklist register; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — confirms ally roster for the appearance
text: Crab and Whimsy on the appearance. Confirmed.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; logistical; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — hearing time and courtroom
text: Hearing at fourteen hundred. Courtroom four.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; matches V1.2 courtroom 4; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — closing order-of-issues for the round
text: Order set. Service first. Fair hearing second. Remedy last. We leave at thirteen-thirty.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; canonical round order for V1.4 courtroom; bare close; not final.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 opening; turns the meeting over to Cula; uses 'Doctor Cula' because client is present (audience-conditional address-form)
text: The notice and the docket are in the file. Doctor Cula will lead.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 technical carrier; senior-bar nuance on cooperative reorganisations; legal effect named (Halina did not overclaim)
text: Two reorganisations of the cooperative since then. Nineteen seventy-four and two thousand and four. The chain holds across both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 blunt-procedural carrier; senior-bar nuance on slip request — terse-instructional
text: Producible by request. We will request it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 technical carrier; agreement on chain-clean assessment
text: It is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 technical carrier; agreement after Crab's chain summary
text: It does.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 Pig-shepherding gentle; dry-collegial register, gentleness in phrasing not volume
text: Mr. Pig. A moment. The meeting room.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 Pig-shepherding directional; redirects Pig to printer
text: The printer. Now.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 post-Pig institutional courtesy; 'long-running matter' is dry-archive, not consoling
text: My apologies, Mrs. Sikorska. The Hennessy retainer phone is a long-running matter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 shared content; fee statement; no currency word (per phase-8 §A.5)
text: The fee for the motion and the rescheduled hearing is five thousand. Fixed. It includes the filing, the appearance, and any drafting necessary if the landlord answers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 shared content; retention bring-through (no twenty-minute timing mismatch)
text: The retention is ready for signature. I will bring it through.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 shared content; retention witness-signature direction; future tense ('I will witness')
text: Two signatures. Here and here. I will witness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 8 close; walk-to-Archive cue (digit-form citation per Piotr arbitration)
text: Article 135-bis § 2. The binder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
text: Read.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 opening; agreement to walk Cula through the article
text: I will.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 first clause — invalid-service definition; full first-reference citation merged into rule sentence; knowledge basis is 2019 renewal countersignature (shared evidence, not blunt-procedural slip)
text: Article 135-bis § 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure defines invalid service: the address used must have been altered, renumbered, or otherwise made non-current to the knowledge of the serving party. The renumbering on Mrs. Sikorska's building was in two thousand and fifteen; the notice was sent to the pre-renumbering address eleven years later. The two thousand and nineteen renewal carries the corrected address and was countersigned by the landlord's office. Knowledge is not in serious dispute.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 second clause — motion + 14-day window; floor/file merged via semicolon
text: The second clause sets the motion procedure. A hearing held upon invalid service shall, on motion of the party not properly served, be set aside and rescheduled, provided the motion is filed within fourteen days of actual notice. Mrs. Sikorska received actual notice on the twenty-eighth of April; the fourteenth day is the eleventh of May. The fourteen-day window is the floor; we file Thursday.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 third clause — third-party non-cure; closes landlord's defence preemptively
text: The third clause preempts the obvious defence. The appearance of a third party at the served address shall not be considered as curing service unless that party held the express authority of the served party. The young man at number seven brought the envelope down because it was on his floormat; he was not authorised. The non-cure clause closes the landlord's defence before he reaches it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 narration close — three clauses confirmed; faintly amused ('motion writes itself')
text: All three. The motion writes itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 dwell A — how-found; archival aside; prior partner anonymised (Plotek is white-collar client per bibles); paired declaratives replace 'not the page number' contrastive antithesis
text: The binder has thirty-three years on it. The binder entry for Article 135-bis § 2 was added in nineteen ninety-three by a clerk whose name I no longer recall. The pencil annotations along the margin are the prior partner's; he annotated the binder for two decades before he retired. The article sits where it sits because invalid service used to live at Article 132 and was relocated when the procedural code was re-paginated. I find it by the pencil. Page numbers change with the binder edition.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 dwell B — used-before; prior-case logic now consistent with first-clause knowledge requirement (landlord knew of redirection)
text: Once. Two thousand and seventeen. A client whose mail had been redirected to a previous tenant's forwarding address. The landlord knew of the redirection and served at the original address regardless. The motion was granted on the third clause — third-party non-cure — because the previous tenant signed for the notice and was not authorised. The court that decided it has since been reorganised. The reasoning is in the pencil annotations along the margin; the case name is not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Murrow
context: Beat 9 dwell C — what-next; sympathetic carrier flavour (Wójcik affidavit lead); active-voice 'We call Mrs. Sikorska'; no slip cross-reference (sympathetic-only bonus)
text: Mrs. Wójcik's statement on the witness affidavit form. Friday afternoon. Mr. Crab will draft the affidavit and you will take it. The motion drafted by Wednesday for filing Thursday. You will draft the application of the rule to the facts; Mr. Crab will draft the service-and-knowledge appendix; Mr. Whimsy will draft the third-party non-cure defence preemption.
